pattern: BFS with state (row, col, remaining_eliminations) finds the shortest path because each state transition is one step; visit each (row, col, k) at most once to stay in O(mnk) time.
hints: BFS on a plain grid finds the shortest path in unweighted graphs; here the state must also record how many obstacle eliminations remain, since arriving at the same cell with more remaining budget is strictly better.

Enqueue (0, 0, k) initially; at each cell try all four neighbors — if it is empty move freely, if it is an obstacle spend one elimination; skip states already visited with at least as many eliminations remaining.
If you ever reach (m-1, n-1) the current BFS level is the answer; if the queue empties without reaching the target return -1.

edge_inputs.description: k=0 and grid is fully blocked except the border route
k large enough to eliminate every obstacle; optimal is Manhattan distance
spending eliminations on a detour is worse than taking the longer free path

stress.description: moderate grid with random obstacles and generous k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(2, size)
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    grid[0][0] = 0
    grid[m-1][n-1] = 0
    k = rng.randint(1, m * n)
    return [grid, k]
